steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the sentences
steps.content: Sentence 1: Guard cells help regulate carbon dioxide intake. Sentence 2: Guard cells help regulate water loss. Both sentences describe separate, equal-weight functions of the same cells. Neither qualifies, contradicts, nor concludes the other — the second simply adds another role alongside the first.

steps.title: Test each transition word against that relationship
steps.content:
• Choice A — In conclusion,: Signals a final summary that wraps up prior reasoning. The second sentence is not a conclusion drawn from the first; it is a parallel fact. Violates the additive relationship. ✗
  • Choice B — Additionally,: Signals that another piece of information of equal weight is being added. Regulating water loss is a second, separate function alongside regulating CO2 — exactly an additive relationship. ✓
  • Choice C — Instead,: Signals contrast or replacement — that the second idea substitutes for the first. Guard cells do both things; regulating water loss does not replace regulating CO2. ✗
  • Choice D — Previously,: Signals a time shift to an earlier point. No temporal contrast exists between these two sentences. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
